'''Kevin Larroyer''' (born 19 June 1989) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a [[Rugby league positions#Second-row|second-row forward]] for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He previously played for [[Toulouse Olympique]], [[Catalans Dragons]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Halifax Panthers]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]].

==Background==
Larroyer was born in [[Toulouse]], [[France]].

==Club career==
[[File:Kevin Larroyer Halifax.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Larroyer with the Halifax Panthers in 2023]]

===Toulouse Olympique===
Larroyer began his professional career with [[Toulouse Olympique]].

===Catalans Dragons===
He moved to the [[Catalans Dragons]] for the 2012 season.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In 2013, Larroyer was signed by Hull Kingston Rovers from [[Catalans Dragons]] on a season long loan deal.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24670158|title=Hull KR bring in Catalan (sic) Dragons forward Kevin Larroyer on loan|newspaper=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref> After an impressive season at Hull Kingston Rovers, making 22 appearances for the Robins and scoring seven tries the club extended Larroyer's loan deal for another year for the [[2015 Super League season|2015 season]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/28887970|title=Hull KR extend loan deal for Catalan (sic) Dragons' Kevin Larroyer|newspaper=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref>

In September 2015, the French international committed his future to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] by signing a three-year deal taking his service with the Robins into 2018.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/larroyer-signs-new-hull-kr-deal-1812026|title=Larroyer signs new Hull KR deal|newspaper=Yorkshire Post|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|first=David|last=Craven|date=27 September 2015}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
He was released from his contract following Hull Kingston Rovers' relegation from Super League at the end of the [[2016 Super League season|2016 season]] and subsequently joined [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2017.<ref name="Larroyer signs new Hull KR deal">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/38985497|title=Kevin Larroyer: Castleford sign former Hull KR forward on one-year deal|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=15 February 2017}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
He spent time on loan at the [[Bradford Bulls]] to regain match fitness early in the 2017 season.<ref name="Larroyer signs new Hull KR deal"/>

===Leigh Centurions===
Larroyer joined Leigh on 17 October 2017 on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41666620|title=Kevin Larroyer: Leigh Centurions sign France forward on two-year deal|publisher=BBC Sport|date=18 October 2017|access-date=8 December 2017}}</ref>

===Halifax===
In 2019, he signed a contract to join [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]], and a one-year contract extension in October 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/im-feeling-good-and-ready-have-strong-season-larroyer-agrees-new-deal-fax-2999557/|title="I'm feeling good and ready to have a strong season" - Larroyer agrees new deal with Fax|website=www.halifaxcourier.co.uk}}</ref> In 2023, Larroyer played in Halifax's [[2023 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] final victory at [[Wembley Stadium|Wembley]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/66463823|title=1895 Cup final: Batley Bulldogs 10-12 Halifax Panthers|publisher=BBC Sport|date=13 August 2023|access-date=8 December 2017}}</ref>

===Hunslet===
In November 2024, Larroyer signed with newly-promoted [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] of the [[RFL Championship]] for the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rugby-league-club-sign-french-veteran-hunslet-rlfc-recruit-ex-castleford-tigers-hull-kr-forward-4859451|first=Peter|last=Smith|title=Hunslet RLFC recruit ex-Castleford Tigers & Hull KR forward|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=8 November 2024|access-date=10 November 2024}}</ref>

==International career==
Larroyer made his senior debut for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in June 2012, appearing from the bench against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in a mid-season international at the [[Racecourse Ground]], Wrexham.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/18396308|title=Wales vs France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=16 June 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wales-vs-france/report/35865|title=France seal composed win|website=Sky Sports|date=16 June 2012|access-date=10 November 2024}}</ref> Later that year, he appeared in all France's matches in the [[2012 Autumn International Series]], including another win over Wales<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/wales/19957505|title=Autumn internationals: France 20-6 Wales|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=20 October 2012}}</ref> and two losses to [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/20156508|title=Autumn International Series: England 44-6 France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=3 November 2012|first=Elizabeth|last=Hudson}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/20258527|title=Autumn International Series: England 48-4 France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=11 November 2012|first=Matthew|last=Newsum}}</ref>

In 2013 Larroyer was selected to represent his home country in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24467856|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Squad lists for all 14 nations|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref> He started in all three group stage matches, a win over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]],<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24695088|first=Shannon|last=Hafez|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Papua New Guinea 8-9 France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=27 October 2023}}</ref> and losses to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24779400|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: New Zealand 48-0 France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=1 November 2023}}</ref> and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24905397|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: France lose to set up England tie|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=11 November 2013|first=Jonathan|last=Jurejko}}</ref>

Larroyer earned three more caps at the [[2014 Rugby League European Championship|2014 European Championship]]. During a 22-12 defeat by [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in [[Tallaght Stadium]], Dublin, Larroyer scored his first international try.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/29674991|title=European Championship: Ireland 22-12 France|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref>

During 2015, Larroyer again earned three caps for France in the [[2015 Rugby League European Championship|European Championship]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_19860-france-name-strong-squad-for-european-championship.html|title=France name strong squad for European Championship|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref> scoring the final try in a 31-14 victory over Ireland in Albi<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.rte.ie/sport/rugby/2015/1017/735538-ireland-fall-to-france-in-rugby-league/|title=Ireland fall to France in Rugby League|date=17 October 2015|newspaper=RTÉ|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024}}</ref> and scoring again in the 32-18 victory over [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in [[Parc des Sports d'Avignon|Avignon]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/656/france-32-18-scotland|title=France 32-18 Scotland|website=European Rugby League|access-date=10 November 2024|date=7 November 2015}}</ref> A fourth international appearance came in a 84-4 defeat to England at [[Leigh Sports Village|Leigh]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/34619482|first=Shannon|last=Hafez|title=England 84-4 France: John Bateman shines in warm-up win|website=BBC Sport|language=en|access-date=10 November 2024|date=24 October 2015}}</ref>

In 2016 Larroyer was selected to play for his country in France's lone international against England in Avignon.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Four Nations warm-up: France 6-40 England|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/37694125|website=BBC Sport|language=en|date=22 October 2016|access-date=10 November 2024}}</ref>
